behavior_summary: Provides the response when the player 'looks at' or examines the CANOPYEW object: it displays a single short inspect message ('canopy ') and then suspends the process.
No branching, no menus, no input capture, and no reads/writes of global flags or external calls — the routine is a minimal U8P_CANOPYEW::look/examine handler.
The displayed text contains a trailing space which appears to be a formatting or concatenation artifact rather than meaningful punctuation.
notable_control_flow: U8P_CANOPYEW::look runs as a process (process type 0x0208) triggered by the player examine/U8P_CANOPYEW::look action.
Execution is linear: emit a Bark (player-facing short text), then suspend/stop the process — there are no conditionals or subsequent actions.
open_questions: Is the trailing space in the bark text ('canopy ') intentionally left for concatenation with other text at runtime, or is it an artifact?
Is this object used elsewhere by scripts that expect a longer description or supplemental text to be appended at runtime?
Are there any fast-area enter/leave handlers for this object (enterFastArea/leaveFastArea) not present in this extract that would affect examine behavior?

main_function.name: U8P_CANOPYEW::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the player's 'U8P_CANOPYEW::look' / examine action for the CANOPYEW object by showing a single short inspect message and suspending the process.
entry_conditions: Invoked when the player performs a 'U8P_CANOPYEW::look' / examine action on the CANOPYEW object (U8P_CANOPYEW::look-type process, process type 0x0208).

function_map.name: U8P_CANOPYEW::look
function_map.role: examine handler (emit short description/bark and suspend)
